pattern: Breadth-first search over indices, where landing on a prime value opens a free hop to every multiple of that prime, with each prime's bucket consumed once to keep the search linear.
hints: Minimum jumps with unit-cost edges is a breadth-first search; the only twist is the extra teleport edges.
From a prime value p you can reach every index whose value is a multiple of p, so pre-group indices by the primes dividing their value.
Run BFS from index 0: neighbours are i-1, i+1, and if nums[i] is prime, all indices in that prime's bucket; clear a bucket after first use so it is never re-expanded.

edge_inputs.description: prime present but no other multiple exists

stress.description: medium array mixing primes and composites with small values
stress.note: small value range so many shared prime factors create teleports

complexity.time: O(n log V + V log log V)
